notes.note: Cicero's wife, who remained at Rome to manage the family's affairs and defend its property during the exile. Her thanks to Atticus acknowledge his help to the household in Cicero's absence.

notes.anchor: My enemies have taken from me my own things, not myself
notes.note: A Stoic-flavoured consolation tag: external goods (property, status) can be stripped away, but the self --- character and mind --- cannot. The 'enemies' are Clodius and the hostile consuls who engineered the exile.

notes.anchor: Sent the fourth day before the Ides of April
notes.note: A Roman date: the fourth day (counted inclusively) before the Ides of April, i.e. 10 April.
Thurii was a town on the gulf of Tarentum in Lucania (southern Italy), a stage on Cicero's flight toward Brundisium and the crossing into exile.
